EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A landspout tornado was reported and photographed over the South Fork area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The public sent images of a weak short-lived landspout tornado over an empty field. Duration is estimated at 10 seconds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Eureka Airport measured severe winds from a thunderstorm in the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Station (KP68) Eureka Airport, Elevation 5945 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Multiple locations experienced heavy rain, flooding, and debris flows due to slow-moving training storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: At least 15 different debris flows were caused by previous thunderstorms/heavy rain during the afternoon. Multiple reports came from the US Forest Service, Elko County Road Department, and Nevada Department of Transportation. USFS 660 will be closed until further notice. The height of debris flows is estimated to be between 4 and 8 feet within a 4-mile length of road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moving across Elko and Eureka county on the afternoon of August 21st caused high winds and severe gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Station (UP994), 3.1 SE West Wendover, Elevation 4408 feet. High sustained winds due to line of thunderstorms moving through.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Station (COMN2) Combs Canyon raws, Elevation 6589 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Early season cold front brought scattered showers and a few thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm outflows to 55 MPH impacted the Reno-Tahoe International Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving Pacific storm moved through the region from the 4th into the 5th bringing strong winds to mainly wind-prone areas of western Nevada with light mountain snow focused in the Lake Tahoe Basin and the northern Carson Range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Gusts between 59-62 MPH were measured at NDOT sites near Schurz and Walker Lake.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving Pacific storm moved through the region from the 4th into the 5th bringing strong winds to mainly wind-prone areas of western Nevada with light mountain snow focused in the Lake Tahoe Basin and the northern Carson Range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Gusty south-southwest winds mainly 40 to 55 mph were felt along the lower valleys of the Basin and Range of western Nevada. The highest measured wind gusts were 60 MPH and 59 MPH along the Black Rock Desert and near Toulon, respectively.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving Pacific storm moved through the region from the 4th into the 5th bringing strong winds to mainly wind-prone areas of western Nevada with light mountain snow focused in the Lake Tahoe Basin and the northern Carson Range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong southwest winds impacted the eastern portion of the Lake Tahoe Basin and the Carson Range with several wind gusts 40 to 55 mph measured. Wind gusts up to 48 to 54 mph were measured along the surface of Lake Tahoe. Rough lake conditions were noted.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving Pacific storm moved through the region from the 4th into the 5th bringing strong winds to mainly wind-prone areas of western Nevada with light mountain snow focused in the Lake Tahoe Basin and the northern Carson Range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Light to moderate snow affected the Lake Tahoe region with up to 8 inches at Mount Rose Ski, 4 inches at the Big Meadows SNOTEL, and 1 inch of snow at Diamond Peak Ski.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Unusually moist airmass associated with a monsoon surge produce widespread thunderstorms. Flash flooding and debris flows occurred as a result, including on very recent burn scars.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Spotter reported 4-6 inches of water running where Fish Springs and Jacobsen Lane meet. Nearby Pine Nut Creek full due to runoff on the Numbers Fire burn scar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Unusually moist airmass associated with a monsoon surge produce widespread thunderstorms. Flash flooding and debris flows occurred as a result, including on very recent burn scars.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nevada DOT reported flooding across US 95 east of Hawthorne at mile marker 40 and flooding across US 95 between Mina and 9 miles north of Mina. The road was closed near Mina due to flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure nearby with enhanced moisture aloft brought scattered slow moving and outflow dominated thunderstorms to the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Spotter reported flooding on some roadways in the Fernley area due to thunderstorms. 0.25 inch of rainfall in 15 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure nearby with enhanced moisture aloft brought scattered slow moving and outflow dominated thunderstorms to the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minor flooding in the Davis Creek Campground due to heavy rainfall from slow moving thunderstorms. 1.2 inches of rainfall in about an hour from a spotter.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Respectable monsoon moisture pushed into the region this week with a large high centered over the Four Corners and Rockies.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nevada 359 closed due to flooding and debris from CA-NV border to 18 miles into Nevada.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Respectable monsoon moisture pushed into the region this week with a large high centered over the Four Corners and Rockies.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Dust storm in Hawthorne coinciding with a thunderstorm wind gust to 56 MPH.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front made a dash for northeast CA and western NV as it got a push from a sharp upper low. This storm was largely moisture starved with high winds the main impact.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Little Valley RAWS hit 80 MPH peak gust. 58 MPH at nearby Galena.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Light snow amounts of 2 to 4 inches in the Lake Tahoe basin.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A northeast wind event mainly affected the ridges of the Carson Range and Lake Tahoe as an area of low pressure dropped southward through the Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The mid-to-upper elevations of the northern Carson Range experienced north and easterly wind gusts from 45 to 79 mph. The 79 mph gust occurred atop Slide Mountain. At the surface of Lake Tahoe, winds gusted from 42 to 54 mph with areas along the eastern shore experiencing gusts 35 to 45 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak, fast-moving winter system moved through western Nevada bringing sparse reports of light snow accumulation from Sparks, the northern Carson Range, and northeastern Carson City the 22nd into the 23rd.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Light snowfall accumulations of 1 to 2 inches were noted along the northern portion of the Carson Range from Mount Rose Ski to Diamond Peak Ski.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak, fast-moving winter system moved through western Nevada bringing sparse reports of light snow accumulation from Sparks, the northern Carson Range, and northeastern Carson City the 22nd into the 23rd.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sparse light snowfall accumulations up to a half-inch were observed 1-mile north-northeast of Sparks and 1 mile northeast of Carson City. No large impacts were noted.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very strong atmospheric river combined with a cold, dynamic area of low pressure produced heavy snowfall across the western Nevada Sierra Front with much of the event focused near and west of the US 395 corridor. Several businesses and schools were closed during the event.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In the Lake Tahoe Basin, snow totals were 2 to 4 feet above 7000 feet with 12 to 30 inches at lake level. Strong winds were noted especially along the northern part of the Carson Range with Slide Mountain seeing gusts over 70 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast moving cold front brought a shorter duration event of gusty winds on Thursday, 10/04, as well as chances for showers, mainly in northeast California and far northwest Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind recorded from Mesonet station Slide mountain 0.9 E Mount Rose Summit (DRI).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A stronger cold front with a weak/low end Atmospheric River brought gusty winds as well as heavier rain and snow to the Sierra and western NV late Monday PM, Nov 8, through Tuesday AM, Nov 9.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gust of 70 mph recorded by Mesonet station, Slide, 0.9 mi E of Mount Rose Summit (DRI).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very strong atmospheric river combined with a cold, dynamic area of low pressure produced heavy snowfall across the western Nevada Sierra Front with much of the event focused near and west of the US 395 corridor. Several businesses and schools were closed during the event.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall varied from little to no valley accumulation due to mixing with rain to as much as 2 to 12 inches, with the highest totals occurring in southwest Reno and in Gardnerville, where as much as 18 to 24 inches of snow fell. Truckee Meadows Community College, Washoe County, and Carson City school districts were closed the 27th. COVID-19 vaccine clinics were closed in Virginia City. There were also power outages reported across Reno and Carson City, mainly on the 27th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving Pacific storm moved through the region from the 4th into the 5th bringing strong winds to mainly wind-prone areas of western Nevada with light mountain snow focused in the Lake Tahoe Basin and the northern Carson Range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong southwest winds impacted western Nevada with widespread gusts 40 to 55 mph felt across many lower valleys. Wind gusts greater than 58 mph were seen in western Reno, Washoe Valley, southwestern Carson City, along US-50 between Dayton and Silver Springs, and along US-395 in the Carson Valley. The highest wind gust was measured atop Peavine Mountain with a reading of 103 mph. Nevada Highway Patrol reported that a 53-foot box trailer was pushed over by the strong winds near Minden during the early afternoon of the 4th. Minor injuries were reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought moderate to heavy snowfall accumulations to western Nevada, which created delays and travel issues for the early week commute the 24th into the 26th. Snow showers continued into the evening hours of the 25th for areas around Reno, with a lake-effect band also occurring over areas southeast of Lake Tahoe including Heavenly Ski Area and areas of the southwestern Carson Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trained Weather Spotter 2 miles south-southeast of Sheridan reported 7.5 inches of lake-effect snowfall during the evening of the 25th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought moderate to heavy snowfall accumulations to western Nevada, which created delays and travel issues for the early week commute the 24th into the 26th. Snow showers continued into the evening hours of the 25th for areas around Reno, with a lake-effect band also occurring over areas southeast of Lake Tahoe including Heavenly Ski Area and areas of the southwestern Carson Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Over the Basin and Range of western Nevada, 4.5 inches of snow fell at Fernley with 3.6 inches falling at Lovelock. Slow going road travel was the main impact.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought moderate to heavy snowfall accumulations to western Nevada, which created delays and travel issues for the early week commute the 24th into the 26th. Snow showers continued into the evening hours of the 25th for areas around Reno, with a lake-effect band also occurring over areas southeast of Lake Tahoe including Heavenly Ski Area and areas of the southwestern Carson Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Along the eastern portion of the Lake Tahoe Basin, 7 inches of snow fell at Heavenly Ski Resort, 6 inches a Diamond Peak Ski Area, and 6 inches at Mount Rose Ski Area. Some lake enhancement likely occurred over the southeastern shore, which contributed to the higher snowfall amount at Heavenly Ski Resort.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought moderate to heavy snowfall accumulations to western Nevada, which created delays and travel issues for the early week commute the 24th into the 26th. Snow showers continued into the evening hours of the 25th for areas around Reno, with a lake-effect band also occurring over areas southeast of Lake Tahoe including Heavenly Ski Area and areas of the southwestern Carson Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Moderate to heavy snowfall was reported across much of the area into the night of the 25th with areas in the North Valleys of Reno measuring 3 to 6.5 inches of snowfall, up to 5 inches in northern Reno, with 4.5 to 6 inches noted across Sparks and Spanish Springs, 2.5 inches in western Reno, 5 to 6.2 inches in south Reno, and up to 10 inches along the southwestern portion of the Carson Valley due to a lake-effect snow band the evening of the 26th. A slow-going commute with delays occurred for many areas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of eastern Pacific winter storm pulses, occasionally aided by induction of warmer sub-tropical moisture, caused a variety of hazardous winter conditions that disrupted holiday travel and commerce over the Sierra and western NV.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The following information was included in High Impact Event report issued 12/26/2021 and 12/27/2021.||Interstate-80 was closed through at least mid-day Monday when the conditions were re-evaluated. Based on the heavy snow forecast Interstate remained closed until Tuesday with no specific estimated opening time. Virtually all trans-Sierra routes are closed due to snow and/or wind impacts - this is also unusual to have so many routes disrupted this long. Caltrans received email briefings from both WFO Reno and WFO Sacramento. ||Southwest Airlines, the largest carrier in Reno, had cancelled all flights today and tonight (12/26/2021), due to wind concerns. Coordination with Reno Airport operations on snow and wind trends. ||Accident involving 20-cars on Interstate 580 between Reno and Carson City with at least 6 injuries requiring hospitalization. Blowing slow occurring at the time of accident. ||Highway 395 in the Eastern Sierra closed for prolonged periods, due to measured wind gusts near the highway of 85 to 98 mph, blowing snow, and low visibility. This is a major route between LA, Mammoth, and Reno. ||All ski areas in the Tahoe region were closed due to dangerous conditions and high winds. Massive impacts likely to people on holiday who may now be stranded at these resorts possibly into Monday and Tuesday. ||Several avalanche reports in the Eastern Sierra but no damage or injuries reported. One occurred near Alpine Meadows closing the only road in and out of the ski area.|An avalanche occurred in the Tahoe area, closing CA 89 which is the main route from Tahoe to I-80. This along with the continued hazardous snowfall and winds, had resulted in the closure of all ski areas in the Tahoe-Truckee region through Monday (12/27/2021).

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Reno 88D radar on Virginia Peak had been without commercial power for roughly 4 days. A visit to the site by electronics staff via snowcat on Christmas Day accessed there was ample generator fuel for about 5 additional days. WFO Reno continued to work with NV Energy on getting commercial service restored, including reaching out to their emergency manager.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm that originated the Gulf of Alaska, thrust into the Western United States from DEC 13-14 and brought heavy snow to the Sierra above 7000'. Accompanying high winds and widespread heavy rainfall to lower elevation areas over parts of eastern CA, the Sierra Front, and western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Official NWS Observations, trained spotters, and the public reported heavy snowfall amounts ranging 4.0-8.0 in and around Carson Valley area from Carson City-Minden to include Genoa. Heavy snowfall amounts ranged 3-7 were reported across the Sparks-Reno foothill areas. Lesser snowfall amounts ranging 1-2 were reported lower valleys of the greater Reno-Sparks area.
